observations.recommendation:
1.5. We reiterated our recommendation that the Inventory Committee comply with
the guidelines and procedures in COA Circular No. 2020-006 dated January 31,
2020, for the one-time cleansing of PPE accounts to ensure that the PPE account
balances are verifiable as to existence, condition, and accountability by
observing the following:
1.5.1. Strictly follow the guidelines and procedures for conducting the physical
count of PPE. This includes recognizing PPE items found at the station
and disposing of items that are non-existent or missing. The Accountant
and Property Officer should reconcile their records based on the results
of the actual physical count and make any necessary adjustments;
1.5.2. Ensure that the Inventory Committee has enough members who are
temporarily relieved of all their regular duties to devote their full time to
the physical inventory process until its completion;
1.5.3. Adopt a uniform numbering system for PPE property numbers and
require the Property Unit to update the property stickers according to
the prescribed format and include all the necessary information as
specified in Sections 5.6 and 5.7 of COA Circular No. 2020-006 dated
January 31, 2020; and
1.5.4. Record and document the physical count daily using the standard ICF
prescribed in Annex A of COA Circular No. 2020-006 dated January 31,
2020.

2.11. We recommended and the Municipal Agriculturist and OIC-MENRO agreed
to:
2.11.1. Conduct an inventory of breeding stocks and plants and trees as of the
end of each year and thereafter render a report including the fair market
value of these items to the Municipal Accountant, for proper recording
in the books of the LGU; and
2.11.2. Keep a systematic record of the Municipality’s breeding stocks and trees,
reporting births, deaths, and disposal, and in case of death and disposal,
facilitate request for relief of accountability in accordance with COA
Circular No. 92-386 dated October 20, 1992, to appropriately drop the
amount from the books.

2.12. We further recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to further
disclose the composition of the Biological Assets account in the Notes to the
Financial Statements in accordance with IPSAS 27 to provide relevant
information that may affect the fairness of the presentation of the FS.

3.11. We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Treasurer to:
3.11.1. Strictly comply with Items 1 and 4 of Section 69 of P.D. No. 1445 and
Section 32 of the NGAS Manual for LGUs by ensuring that all collections
are deposited intact with the AGDB at the end of each day or within the
prescribed period;
3.11.2. Regularly notify the Collecting Officers to remit their collections at the
end of each working day or at least once a week if they are stationed in
far-flung areas and to establish cut-off dates for collections and deposits,
especially at year-end;
3.11.3. Designate a responsible accountable officer, in accordance with existing
regulations, to deposit collections to the municipality’s AGDB within the
prescribed period during her absence or when she is not personally
available to do so.

4.11. We recommended that the OIC - Municipal Health Officer comply with the
requirements under DOH AO No. 2018-001 dated January 28, 2018, to obtain
the DOH-LTO for the four vehicles.

5.9. We recommended and the LDRRMO and the LDRRMC agreed to:
5.9.1. Include PPAs that are chargeable against the prior years’ unexpended
LDRRMF in the annual LDRRMFIP of the current year, in accordance
with the format prescribed under Annex A of COA Circular No. 2012-
002; and
5.9.2. Establish an efficient review and monitoring system to ensure that the
LDRRMFIP complies with the requirements outlined in COA Circular
No. 2012-002.

6.5. We recommended that LDRRMO-Designate:
6.5.1. Adhere strictly to the provisions of DILG Memorandum Circular No.
2012-73 dated April 17, 2012, and NDRRMC-DBM-DILG Joint
Memorandum Circular No. 2013-1 dated March 25, 2013, on the
utilization of the LDRRMF;
6.5.2. Reimburse the LDRRMF from the General Fund for the cost of fuel used
by non-LDRRM vehicles; and
6.5.3. Ensure that the LDRRM motor vehicles and heavy equipment are
exclusively used for disaster/calamity response and rescue/relief
operations in conformity with DILG MC No. 2021-004 dated January 18,
2021. Otherwise, refund or replenish the LDRRMF for the cost of the
subject motor vehicles and heavy equipment, the appropriations for
which shall be taken from the General Fund.

to enforce collection of delinquent real property taxes by conducting an intensive tax
campaign in every barangay, and by availing of the remedies in collecting delinquent
real property taxes provided under Sections 254, 256, and 258 of R.A. No. 7160.

We recommended and Management agreed to submit the required additional
documentations enumerated above for further audit validation. Pending compliance
thereto, audit action on this transaction is hereby suspended, and a Notice of
Suspension shall accordingly be issued.
We likewise recommended that Management adhere strictly to the provisions
of Section 4 of P.D. 1445 and COA Circular No. 2012-001 on documentary
requirements before effecting any payments, and see to it that all supporting
documents are complete and attached together with the voucher.

We recommended and the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal
Treasurer agreed to review and validate the recorded Accounts Payable amounting
to ₱656,323.27 to ascertain if these still represent valid claims to the extent possible,
otherwise seek authority from the Sangguniang Bayan for the Mayor to request for
write-off of the account balances from the COA, duly supported by the requirements
stipulated under Section III.A.10 of COA Circular No. 97-001 so that these may be
made available for funding valuable development programs and other priority
activities and so that the liabilities would be fairly presented in the financial
statements. We further recommended that the Municipal Accountant review the
remaining A/P balance and determine the validity of the recorded claims, otherwise
include the undocumented amounts in the request for write-off.

We recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to exhaust all
available remedies to secure the documents pertaining to the animals as reported in
the books, with sufficient documentation of the validation undertaken. We further
recommended that the Municipal Agriculturist verify the status of the animals, its
conditions and present caretaker, through physical inspection, update the fair market
values, and submit the corresponding report, which could also serve as basis to
support a possible request for write-off, if warranted under the circumstances.

We strongly recommended and Management agreed to ensure that all
accountable officers of the LGU, especially Job Order personnel, shall always be
properly bonded pursuant to the provisions in Section 101 (2) of P.D. No. 1445 and
Item 4.1.2 of Treasury Circular No. 02-2019 dated April 25, 2019.

We recommended and Management agreed to require the HRMO head to:
a. strictly adhere to the provisions of the Omnibus Rules on Leave and
   CSC Resolution No. 34 on the granting and approval of monetization
   of leave credits; and
b. prioritize the meticulous checking, reconciliation and monitoring of the
   employees’ leave cards to reflect the correct leave credit balances and
   to extract accountability from those with excessive monetization claims
   due to the incorrect leave balances.
We further recommended that the employees who monetized their leave
credits in excess of the allowed balances immediately refund the government in
accordance with the Notice of Disallowance which shall be issued accordingly.

We recommended and Management agreed to (a) furnish the Auditor copies
of the contracts, purchase orders and certificate of acceptance, together with all
supporting documents thereto within the required timeframe; (b) indicate all the
basic information in the purchase order as required under COA Circular Nos. 96-010
and 2009-001 to ensure delivery of items that meet the specification.

We recommended that management give due importance on the physical count by requiring the Municipal Inventory Committee to conduct the physical count of all inventory accounts and prepare the required inventory reports in order to establish the existence and determine the reliability of the account balances shown in the financial statements at year-end. Likewise, we recommended that the General Services Officer and Municipal Accountant maintain the required stock cards and supplies ledger cards for each type of supplies, respectively, pursuant to Sections 119 and 120 of the Manual on the New Government Accounting System for Local Government Units, Volume I.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2020 The Municipal Inventory Committee failed to conduct an inventory of such account due to its nature of being semi-expendable & consumable in nature, thus the GSO was required to issue Requisition & Issuance Slip and Summary of Supplies & Materials Issued Report as basis for the adjusting entry and to present a reliable account balance.
observations.reason: Not Being longer locate the inventory. This observation is reiterated in Findings No. 1 of Part II hereof.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the DSWD personnel submit to the Municipal Accountant the required supporting documents in their accordance with Section 8 of Joint Memorandum Circular No. 1, Series of 2020 and the latter to attach the required documents to the payrolls in order to ensure the validity, propriety and legality thereon. We further recommended that henceforth, the similar releases should be performed by authorized and bonded disbursing officers pursuant to Section 4.3.3 of Treasury Circular No. 2009-02.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2020 The beneficiaries had submitted a certification of need for assistance from DSWD, in lieu of any of the certifications specified in DWSD MC 04 s. 2020. The certification had already been attached to the submitted payroll. The rice farmers included in the sample of SAP recipients with incomplete supporting documents were only laborers. They were not qualified for the Department of Agriculture (DA) Registry System for Basic Sectors in Agriculture (RSBSA) beneficiaries. Their IDs & Certifications had been attached to the submitted payroll. As per recommendation and pursuant to Section 4.3.3 of Treasury Circular No. 2009-02, subsequent similar releases would still be performed by authorized and bonded disbursing officers despite the time pressure set by the guidelines.
observations.reason: Management had only submitted the certifications of the recipient’s need for assistance. The other documentary requirements had not been complied with.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management, particularly the BAC Secretariat and the Municipal Accountant, require the contractors of the foregoing projects to submit a warranty security for structural defects and failures as a supporting document to their respective claims for final payment in accordance with Section 62.2.3.3 of Revised IRR No. 9184.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2020 Management had already secured warranty bonds for projects issued with the Certificate of Final Acceptance not later than one (1) month upon receipt of the AOM. For all ongoing projects, Warranty Security was required prior to final payment.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that in all cases, the Bids and Awards Committee, indicate in the PO all the basic information required under COA Circular No. 96-010 to ensure that items delivered meet the specification. We also recommended that the General Service Office submit the List of Rice Recipients (rice) with signatures acknowledging receipt thereof, Monthly Report of Official Travels summarizing all trip tickets issued for the month and Monthly Report of Fuel Consumption (Fuel), Program of Works and Plans/Designs (Canopies) to ensure the validity, propriety and legality thereof. Further, drivers should indicate necessary information in Drivers Trip Tickets to facilitate evaluation of gasoline/diesel, oil and lubricants procedure. Lastly, we recommended that the LGU submit a written explanation that would sufficiently justify why gasoline and diesel used for regular transactions were charged to BGCM, otherwise the same shall be disallowed in audit.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2020 The rice acquired was locally made & commercially branded as Canlaon Rice. The Monthly Report of Supplies & Materials Issued for consumption and trip tickets for Official Travels had already been submitted on 2/5/2021. For the canopy, a soft copy of the ARE with pictures, and POW had been submitted on 1/27/2021 and 1/29/2021, respectively, through email. For the rice beneficiaries, a soft copy and hard copy had been sent last 2/01/2021 and 2/5/2021, respectively. Management had only two (2) rented storages, one for diesel & one for gasoline. Fuel charged to BGCM had still been used in daily operation however, a replenishment was made after. The fuel for travel was used in COVID-19 response activities.
observations.reason: A written explanation that would sufficiently justify the Municipality’s use of gasoline and diesel that was charged to BGCM for regular transactions was not provided to the Audit Team.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management require the supplier to return the representing excess payment of the 50 pieces LED Tennis Type, Street Lamp at ₱21,990.00, otherwise the bids and awards committee and other signatories of the transaction are jointly and severally liable. We also recommended that the Inspectorate Committee explain why they accepted the lightning materials and certified that the delivery conformed with specifications when the brand was not indicated in the purchase order.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2020 The specifications, together with the brand name and the simulation result of the Vallehermoso LED Streetlight project had already been submitted on 08/20/2021. The Street Lights purchased were 19500 lumens not 1950 lumens.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Treasurer to verify from the records of the BIR or from audited financial statements the accuracy of the declared gross receipts of the taxpayers in order to assess the correct amount of tax and correct payment thereof from the business establishments.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2020 In CY 2022, the accuracy of the taxpayer’s declared gross receipts had been verified to assess the correct amount of taxes through audited financial statements, if applicable, especially those taxpayers who are LGU bidders/suppliers.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer adopt a more aggressive tax collection policy to include posting in the municipal and barangay halls a list of delinquent real property taxpayers, issuance of tax bills to be sent out before December of each year, tapping the barangays for assistance in the distribution thereof, and to consider recommending to the Mayor and the Sangguniang Bayan the sale of the delinquent properties through public auction.
observations.managementAction: 2019 Implemented The LGU was still on process of manually updating and computing tax bills because of the damaged computer used in the iTax system. The tax bills and notices of delinquency (as of December 2021) had been scheduled to be sent out in the third quarter of CY 2022, with assistance from the concerned barangay in the distribution thereof.
observations.reason: Not completed as of December 2021 and due for issuance/distribution to delinquent taxpayers had not been sent out due to the COVID-19 pandemic and lack of field officers/workers in the RPT Division.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Agricultural Officer (MAO) review the Memoranda of Agreement of the projects with unused funds in order to determine the provision for the liquidation thereof and to immediately remit to the source agency the unused funds. We further recommended that they take the initiative of following up the Municipality’s request with the NAPC for the use of the unexpected balance of the Communal Dairy Milk Production for Sustainable Agriculture Program, otherwise the unused funds should likewise be returned to the source agency.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2019 The Accounting Office still needed to find the supporting covering documents before the funds are returned. The LGU had already transmitted to NAPC the request for the fund return.
observations.reason: The lack of supporting documents & also personnel hindered the Accounting Office from identifying the source agency. Since the proposal submitted had not been acted upon due to the pandemic lockdown.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the personnel who signed the Inspection and Acceptance Report submit a detailed list of the live weight of each carabao inspected, and to explain why they accepted the animals which had not reached the minimum age of two years.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2018 The livelihood project had been fully implemented and the intent of providing benefits to the marginalized farmers had not been inhibited.
observations.reason: The available documents had not been submitted yet. The age requirement was commensurate with the sizes of the carabaos delivered.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor fill up the mandatory positions of Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Planning and Development Officer as required under Sections 470 and 576 of R.A. 7160, respectively. We also recommended that designations if any, be limited to only one year and that the designation of any personnel should only be within the level he is currently occupying, as provided under existing regulations.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2018 Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Planning and Development Officer positions had been permanently filled up since September 1, 2019, and December 16, 2021, respectively.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant review the 2003 journals and ledgers to identify the local government units to whom the amounts had been transferred to and to demand liquidation thereof. If these efforts prove futile, the municipality may request for the write-off of the receivables in accordance with COA Circular No. 2016-005 dated December 19, 2016. We further recommended that henceforth, the municipal accountant faithfully maintain subsidiary ledgers for all control accounts as required under the Manual on NGAS.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2018 The Accounting Office reviewed the CY 2003 journals and ledgers but had not identified the fund transfer’s recipient LGUs as transaction records remained unlocated. Hence, Management planned to write a letter to COA requesting for write-off of the accounts.
observations.reason: No records are available, both in the accounting & treasurer’s offices.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Bids and Awards Committee and the Municipal Mayor submit an explanation for awarding the contract to the contractor despite its lack of necessary equipment for use in the projects, and for not terminating the contracts despite the lapse of more than a year after the contract period expired. We also recommended that the municipal mayor consider terminating the projects, forfeiting the contractor’s performance bonds, take over the projects or award the same to the other qualified contractors through negotiated procurement as provided under Sec. 8.4 of Annex E of the Revised IRR of RA 9184. Lastly, we recommended that the municipality demand the return of the advance payments otherwise the administrative and judicial remedies provided under the law should be imposed.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2018 The project was completed on December 7, 2019.
observations.reason: The LCE’s letter of explanation, together with the project termination letter, had not yet been submitted to the Audit Team.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipality insure the project and equipment with the General Insurance Fund of the Government Service Insurance System (GSIS) in order that the government may be indemnified from any damage to, or loss of the project in case of fire, earthquake, storm, or other calamity. We also recommended that the General Services Officer prepare and issue Acknowledgement Receipts for Equipment to end-users of the tablets, power banks, printer and desk top computers so that accountability therefor can be established immediately.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2018 The ₱5.534 million were capital outlay purchases of the Trust Fund and recorded in the Trust Fund. For the equipment, CY 2012 and 2023 DVs & Journals had already been submitted.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that payment of honoraria in violation of existing rules and regulations be immediately stopped and that the officials and personnel who received payment in violation of regulations be required to refund the amounts collected upon receipt of the Notices of Disallowance. We further recommended that henceforth, the municipality observe existing rules and regulations on the grant of honoraria.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2017 Management had immediately stopped the payment of additional compensation to the concerned personnel.
observations.reason: Refunds had not yet been made due to non-receipt of Notice of Disallowance.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management issue demand letters to incumbent and former officials and employees with unliquidated cash advances otherwise withhold amounts from the salaries of incumbent personnel. In addition, require the Municipal Accountant and other concerned officials/employees to strictly observe the provisions of COA Circular No. 97-002 in the grant, utilization and liquidation of cash advances.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2017 Only the deceased & retired employees’ cash advances remained dormant for the aging more than 10 years & last 3 years, but demand letters had been sent twice a year for the retirees, including those aging 10 years & above. A request for write-off would be made for the cash advance balance of the deceased former employees.
observations.reason: We failed to request for write off of the dormant accounts more than 10 years & those of the deceased.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the OIC-Municipal Treasurer monitor the municipality’s depository accounts to ensure that the required minimum balance is maintained in order to avoid any service charges. We further recommended that the Municipal Treasurer refund to the municipality the bank service charges of ₱3,630.00.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2020 Municipal Treasurer had already refunded the bank Service charge in CY 2021 while the said bank account was already closed per SB Resolution No. 2020-149.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Finance Committee study and explore possibilities of increasing local revenues in order to reduce the municipality’s dependence on the internal revenue allotment to finance its operations. We further recommended that the Municipal Treasurer impose the taxes on sugar plantations located in the LGU as provided by law.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2017 As provided by law, the Treasurer's Office had already collected taxes from sugar plantations.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the: a) Inventory Committee give priority to the conduct of the physical count guided by the regulations prescribed under Section 490 of the GAAM, Volume I, reconcile the results with the accounting records, prepare the report for submission to our office and pinpoint responsibility for missing properties; b) General Services Officer secure all copies of the ARE and update the filing thereof, ensuring that these are principally considered especially during the separation of accountable officers from the government service; c) Municipal Accountant invariably require that the ARE be fully complied with, in form and quantity, as an indispensable supporting document for purchases of movable PPE; d) Management submits the schedule of PPE additions and disposals from CYs 2014-2016, complete with data on the property tag, ARE, accountable officer, office/location of the equipment, and the actual DVs and ARE; otherwise, those that cannot be accounted for shall be accordingly disallowed; and e) Municipal Accountant attach the formatted and detailed schedule of PPE additions and disposals to the yearly financial report of the LGU and regularly update it to facilitate the conduct of the physical inventory and to pinpoint responsibility for government properties.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2016 The 6.64 million represents the movable property purchases and from CY 2014 to December 31, 2021, all purchases had ARE. Management also inspected and conducted inventory of some movable properties like Office Equipment & Motor vehicles. A PPE schedule had already been prepared.
observations.reason: The Inventory Committee had already planned to conduct a physical count and ensure its submission before the fiscal year’s end.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management desist from charging to government funds expenditures related to beauty pageants and fiesta activities, reception and entertainment, exercise caution in the allocation and utilization of government funds for expenditures related to fiestas or religious activities, ensuring that regulations for the prevention of irregular and unnecessary expenditures are observed. We further recommended the immediate refund of the payments made for the beauty pageants as these are disallowed in audit.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2016 The LGU had already ceased charging expenditures for beauty pageants, fiesta activities. Management planned to contact the person responsible to collect a refund.
observations.reason: The persons responsible no longer connected with LGU-Vallehermoso.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management discontinue deducting from the payroll any payment to the aforementioned private financing institutions, except for loans availed by the employees prior to receipt of this Audit Observation Memorandum, until such loans shall have been fully paid and, henceforth, desist from entering into similar contracts with financing institutions not otherwise authorized under the GAA.
observations.managementAction: AAR The employees were no longer granted additional loans, resulting in the increase in their net take home pay in compliance with regulations.
observations.reason: The LGU still availed of loans from DCCCO & CFI.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management to submit the official acknowledgement by the Senior Citizens of the amount of ₱88,320.00 that was released to them as their share of the La Playa gate entrance fees. We further recommended that accountability for collections made at the La Playa should be directly entrusted to personnel under the Municipal Treasurer’s Office, that cash tickets should be used invariably in exchange for the ₱3.00 fee collected therefrom, and cease from using unofficial accountable forms in acknowledgement of all funds received by the collectors in their official capacity.
observations.managementAction: AAR The ₱88,320.00 were already entrusted to the Municipal Treasurer’s Office personnel. On the other hand, the Senior Citizen’s share had already been stopped for lack of basis.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant submit the financial records (JEVs, DVs, Journals) substantiating the adjustment made. Henceforth, closely monitor the correctness and completeness of entries made for the procurement of PPE, especially those in the Trust Fund, in accordance with generally accepted accounting principles.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2016 The ₱5.534 million were capital outlay purchases of the Trust Fund and recorded in the Trust Fund without capitalizing the said transactions. CY 2012 and 2023 DVs & Journals had already been submitted.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management require the Municipal Assessor, as well as, the Municipal Treasurer to convene the Provincial Appraisal Committee to immediately act in the determination of the value of the unrecorded lots. Also direct them to process the titling of the lots owned by the municipality and for the Municipal Budget Officer to allocate funds for the titling thereof. Since the remaining Municipal-owned lots were still subject to appraisal, Management shall again request the PAC to facilitate the completion of the lot’s appraisal. Management shall again record in the books of accounts those lots that are owned by the municipality and the value of the lots which will be determined by the Provincial Appraisal Committee shall also be recorded in the books of accounts to avoid understatement of assets.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2015 The Provincial Appraisal Committee had already conducted an appraisal on the prioritized site/lot, particularly the Municipal Public Market Site at Brgy. Poblacion. The Assessor’s Office had already prepared the pertinent documents relative to the status of these lots.
observations.reason: A request to the Provincial Appraisal Committee to conduct value appraisal on the prioritized subject lots will be made in the 3rd quarter of CY 2022.

observations.recommendation: Require the municipal accountant to make necessary adjustment to reconcile the Due to Other Funds with the Due From Other Funds.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2015 The Accounting Office had already exerted efforts to reconcile the accounts.
observations.reason: The unreconciled balance pertains to previous years, the records of which have yet to be secured.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Engineer and Treasurer impose liquidated damages on the delayed implementation of projects as provided under the 2016 Revised IRR of R.A. 9184 in order to compel contractors to complete projects on time as well as to protect the interest of the government.
observations.managementAction: Management agreed to impose liquidated damages; actions are being processed.

observations.managementAction: The BAC will strictly follow that in the Bid Proposal Brand Names should be indicated. The certificate of product registration was submitted in your office through Jordan Bernus for the hard copy and soft copy via Miss Jane Generoso’s email address.

If so, the contractor should be made to construct another spring box which can catch water from the source. Otherwise, the municipality should appropriate funds for the immediate construction of a new spring box in order that the waterworks system may be made functional. observations.recommendation: We recommended that the municipal treasurer intensify collection of real property taxes by conducting tax campaigns, tapping the assistance of the barangay officials. observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Bids and Awards Committee and the Municipal Mayor submit an explanation for awarding the contract to the contractor despite its lack of necessary equipment for use in the projects, and for not terminating the contracts despite the lapse of more than a year after the contract periods expired. We also recommended that the municipal mayor consider terminating the projects, forfeiting the contractor’s performance bonds, take over the projects or award the same to other qualified contractors through negotiated procurement as provided under Sec. 8.4 of Annex E of the Revised IRR of RA 9184. Lastly, we recommended that the municipality demand the return of the advance payments otherwise the administrative and judicial remedies provided under the law should be imposed.
observations.managementAction: The FMR at Brgy. Pinucauan was completed on 2/20/19, while the FMR at Brgy. Guba was completed on 2/20/19. The LGU imposed and collected liquidated damages amounting to ₱50,005.30 deducted to the final payment. The remaining ₱3.194 million in Malangsa was abandoned by contractor and management sent notice of termination last 8/22/2019 and thus compelled to declare the contractor backlisted. The project was pursued by administration and was completed on 12/7/2019 using the balance of unpaid 20% of progress billing plus 80% retention plus unliquidated 20% mobilization amounted to ₱201,115.20 to complete the project.
observations.reason: The Bids and Awards Committee and the Municipal Mayor did not submit to COA an explanation for awarding the contract to the contractor despite its lack of necessary equipment for use in the projects, and for not terminating the contracts despite the lapse of more than a year after the contract periods expired.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipality insure the project and equipment with the General Insurance Fund of the Government Service Insurance System (GSIS) in order that the government may be indemnified from any damage to, or loss of the project in case of fire, earthquake, storm, or other calamity. We also recommended that the General Services Officer prepare and issue Acknowledgement Receipts for Equipment to end-users of the tablets, power banks, printer and desk top computers so that accountability therefor can be established immediately.
observations.managementAction: ARE was issued to end-users of computer equipment purchased.
observations.reason: Lack of funds hindered the LGU from insuring the flood control project.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Engineer and Treasurer impose liquidated damages on the delayed implementation of projects as provided under the 2016 Revised Implementing Rules and Regulations of R.A. 9184 in order to compel contractors to complete projects on time as well as to protect the interest of the government.
observations.managementAction: Management had stopped the charging of expenses in connection with the delayed works and insisted to impose the said liquidated damages.
observations.reason: Not Implemented

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor determine whether the error in the selection of the site on which the spring box was constructed was attributable to the contractor. If so, the contractor should be made to construct another spring box which can catch water from the source. Otherwise, the municipality should appropriate funds for the immediate construction of a new spring box in order that the waterworks system may be made functional. We further recommended that the Mayor secure the donation papers for the lot on which the powerhouse was constructed.
observations.managementAction: As to donation papers all are in 2017 already inspected every household in 15 brgys of 100% compliance on 10/21/2020. The LGU attempted to construct another spring box but water supply is insufficient.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that payment of honoraria in violation of existing rules and regulations be immediately stopped or stopped and that the officials and personnel who received payment in violation of regulations be required to refund the amounts collected upon receipt of the Notices of Disallowance. We further recommended that henceforth, the municipality observe existing rules and regulations on the grant of honoraria.
observations.managementAction: The said payments were stopped the management immediately stopped the payment of additional compensation to the concerned personnel.
observations.reason: Refunds not yet stopped the payment of additional compensation to the concerned personnel.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the management issue demand letters to incumbent and former officials and employees with unliquidated cash advances otherwise withhold the amounts from the salaries of incumbent personnel. In addition, require the Municipal Accountant and concerned officials/employees to refund. We further recommended that they observe the provisions of COA Circular No. 97-002 in the grant, utilization and liquidation of cash advances.
observations.managementAction: The accounting office issued demand letters twice a year and stopped the payment. The municipal office required the officials to refund unliquidated cash advances.
observations.reason: Despite the issuance of demand letters to concerned officials, unliquidated cash advances remain significant.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the municipality consider taking on the cost of the supposed counterpart of the residents by appropriating the funds to complete the project in order that its objectives of providing sanitary toilets and improving the health and sanitation of their constituents will be achieved.
observations.managementAction: Management agreed to take on the cost and to appropriate the funds to complete the project and the projects were completed.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the OIC-Municipal Treasurer monitor the municipality’s depository accounts to ensure that the required minimum balance is maintained in order to avoid any service charges. We further recommended that the Municipal Treasurer refund to the municipality the bank service charges of ₱3,630.00.
observations.managementAction: The depositary account already closed on 12/22/2020. The Municipal Treasurer gradually paid the bank service charges.
observations.reason: Bank service charges was paid gradually by the Municipal Treasurer.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Finance Committee study and explore possibilities of increasing local revenues in order to reduce the municipality’s dependence on the internal revenue allotment to finance its operations. We further recommended that the Municipal Treasurer impose the taxes on sugar plantations located in the LGU as provided by law.
observations.managementAction: Management enacted revenue code effective 2020 which covered the taxes on sugar plantation.
observations.reason: Lack of adequate knowledge and personnel to conduct the inventory taking and to fully implement the revenue code.

observations.recommendation: a) Inventory Committee give priority to the conduct of physical count guided by the regulations of 2016 to present ARE and inspected and reconcile the results thereof with the accounting records, prepare the report for submission to our office and pinpoint responsibility for missing properties; b) General Services Officer secure all copies of the ARE and update the filing thereof, ensuring that these are principally considered especially during the separation of accountable officers from the government service; c) Municipal Accountant require that the movable PPE accounts ARE be fully complied with, in form and quantity, as an indispensable supporting document for purchases of movable PPE; d) Management submits the schedule of PPE additions and disposals from CYs 2014-2016, complete with data on the property tag, ARE, accountable officer, office/location of the equipment, and the actual DVs and ARE; otherwise, those that cannot be accounted for shall be accordingly disallowed; and e) Municipal Accountant attach the formatted and detailed schedule of PPE additions and disposals to the yearly financial report of the LGU and regularly update it to facilitate the conduct of the physical inventory and to pinpoint responsibility for government properties.

The accounting office already set up ARE and inventory accounts. The accounting office is currently working on reconciling the schedules and supporting documents.
observations.reason: Lack of adequate personnel and knowledge to conduct the inventory taking.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and Management, spearheaded by the MENRO and MDRRMO Designated Officers, agreed to fast-track the preparation of solid waste management plans under the environmental management component to provide effective guidance in the implementation of programs outlined therein, and fully undertake measures to comply with the requirements to pass the SGLG assessment in order to access the PCF and incentives package which could provide essential funding for the LGU’s programs.
observations.managementAction: The 34 projects were already implemented in 2019.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Management undertake measures to comply with the requirements and to prepare programs to meet the criteria including fast-tracking the preparation of Solid Waste management plans and other measures to comply with the SGLG requirements.

observations.recommendation: We recommended Management desist from charging to government funds expenditures related to beauty pageants and exercise caution in the allocation and utilization of government funds for expenditures related to fiestas or religious activities, reception and entertainment, ensuring that regulations for the prevention of irregular and unnecessary expenditures are observed. observations.recommendation: We recommended Management replenish the 20% Development Fund from the General Fund through a duly authorized appropriation ordinance and henceforth desist from appropriating the 20% DF for subsidies and maintenance or operating expenses, in compliance with DILG- DBM JMC No.2011-1.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management: a) Evaluate the necessity of granting additional service payments to personnel, giving consideration to the source of fund, the PS cap, employment status for entitlement and the requirements of DBM Budget Circular (BC) No. 2007-2 in case of special projects; b) Formulate and adopt internal rules and procedures on the rendition of overtime services of personnel and ensure that the documentary requirements stipulated under Section 5.10 of COA Circular No. 2012-001 are complied with; c) Refund the payment of extra time services to personnel simultaneously granted with additional services upon determination that the latter is valid, otherwise refund the payment of the latter; and d) Submit the required documents to enable COA to make a decision in audit.
observations.managementAction: Management stopped the additional payments and extra time services. The accounting office is requiring supporting documents and is implementing strict compliance.
observations.reason: Management still did not submit all required supporting documents and legal bases for some payments.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management discontinue deducting from the payroll any loan payment to the aforementioned private financing institutions, except for loans availed by the employees prior to receipt of this Audit Observation until such loans shall have been fully paid and, henceforth, desist from entering into similar contracts with financing institutions not authorized under the law.
observations.managementAction: The employees were no longer granted additional loan that will result to net below the required net take home pay. The LGU already stopped the payroll deductions to DCCCO and CFI.
observations.reason: The LGU still avail loans from DCCCO and CFI.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant submit the financial records (JEVs, DVs, Journals) substantiating the adjustment made. observations.recommendation:
We recommended and the Municipal Treasurer agreed to adopt a more
aggressive tax collection policy to include posting in the municipal and barangay
halls a list of delinquent real property taxpayers, issuance of tax bills to be sent out
before December of each year, tapping the barangays for assistance in the
distribution thereof, and to consider recommending to the Mayor and the
Sanguniang Bayan the sale of delinquent properties through public auction.

We recommended and the Municipal Mayor agreed to request the supplier
to refund the excess payment of ₱1,044,050.00 otherwise the same shall become the
liability of all personnel who participated in the transaction. We further
recommended and the Municipal Inspectorate Team agreed to perform their duties
with due diligence in order to protect the municipality’s interest, and that the
Municipal Accountant correct the accounts affected. Lastly, we recommended and
the Property Officer agreed to require all offices to submit an accounting of the fuel
issued to and used by them through the submission of Monthly Report of Supplies
and Materials Issued, duly supported with Requisition and Issue Slips and Trip
Tickets, to be submitted to the Accountant in order that the Fuel, Oil, and
Lubricants Inventory account previously set up will be adjusted on the basis
thereof.

We recommended and management agreed that henceforth, Bids and
Awards Committee should include in the Instruction to Bidders a provision
requiring the bidder to indicate the brand names of the drugs and medicines being
offered, and that these are supported with Certificates of Product Registration for
evaluation purposes. We further recommended that the BAC-TWG thoroughly
review the documents submitted by the bidder against the Instruction to Bidders
before declaring his bid as “Compliant”. Lastly, we recommended that the
Municipal Accountant see to it that the voucher is supported by the required CPRs
in order to ensure validity of the payment made.

We recommended and the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal
Agricultural Officer (MAO) agreed to review the covering Memoranda of
Agreement of the projects with unused funds in order to determine the provision for
the liquidation thereof and to immediately remit to the source agency the unused
funds. We further recommended that they take the initiative of following up the
Municipality’s request with the NAPC for the use of the unexpended balance of the
Communal Dairy Milk Production for Sustainable Agriculture Program, otherwise
the unused funds should likewise be returned to the source agency.

We recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to draw a journal
entry voucher to transfer the completed project from the Trust Fund to the
General Fund for proper presentation of the financial statements pursuant to
Section 104 of the Manual on NGAS. We further recommended that the Offices of
the Municipal Accountant and General Services maintain property, plant and
equipment ledger cards and property cards, respectively, in accordance with
existing regulations.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor determine whether the error in the selection of the site on which the spring box was constructed was attributable to the contractor. If so, the contractor should be made to construct another spring box which can catch water from the source. Otherwise, the municipality should appropriate funds for the immediate construction of a new spring box in order that the waterworks system may be made functional. We further recommended that the Mayor secure the donation papers for the lot on which the powerhouse was constructed.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2017 No action.
observations.reason: The municipal engineering’s office will still conduct re-evaluation of the project for more appropriate action.

We further recommended that the Municipal Treasurer refund to the municipality the bank service charges of ₱3,630.00.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2017 No action.
observations.reason: The Municipal Treasurer will still request for closure of the account.

observations.managementAction: AAR 2015 Management refrained from appropriating the 20% DF for subsidies and maintenance or operating expenses and is in compliance with DILG-DBM JMC No.2011-1.

observations.recommendation:
RECOMMENDATION:
We recommended that the personnel who signed the Inspection and
Acceptance Report submit a detailed list of the live weight of each carabao
inspected, and to explain why they accepted the animals which had not reached the
minimum age of two years.

RECOMMENDATION:
We recommended that the municipal treasurer intensify the collection of
real property taxes by conducting tax campaigns, tapping the assistance of the
barangay officials. We also recommended that the LGU issue notices of
delinquency and impose remedies provided by law, where applicable.

RECOMMENDATION:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor fill up the mandatory positions
of Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Planning and Development Officer as
required under Sections 470 and 476 of RA. 7160, respectively. We also
recommended that designations if any, be limited to only one year and that the
designation of any personnel should only be within the level he is currently
occupying, as provided under existing regulations.

RECOMMENDATIONS:
We recommended that the Municipal Accountant review the 2003 journals
and ledgers to identify the local government units to whom the amounts had been
transferred to and to demand liquidation thereof. If these efforts prove futile, the
municipality may request for the write-off of the receivables in accordance with
COA Circular No. 2016-005 dated December 19, 2016. We further recommended
that henceforth, the municipal accountant faithfully maintain subsidiary ledgers for
all control accounts as required under the Manual on NGAS.

RECOMMENDATIONS:
We recommended that officials and employees whose premiums remain
unremitted be made to immediately comply with the documentary requirements set
by GSIS so that the premium payments would be finally accepted. We also
recommended that the Municipal Accountant identify members whose GSIS
premiums remain unremitted by tracing entries in the subsidiary ledgers to the
disbursement vouchers/payrolls and to remit the same as soon as possible.

We recommended that the BAC should invariably post-qualification of bidders to ensure that only eligible bidders are allowed to participate in the bidding as required under Sec. 34, Rule X of the 2016 Revised IRR of R.A. 9184.

observations.recommendation:
RECOMMENDATION:
We recommended that the BAC should invariably conduct post-qualification
of bidders to ensure that only eligible bidders are allowed to participate in the
bidding as required under Sec. 34, Rule X of the 2016 Revised IRR of R.A. 9184.

RECOMMENDATION:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor determine whether the error in
the selection of the site on which the spring box was constructed was attributable to
the contractor. If so, the contractor should be made to construct another spring box
which can catch water from the source. Otherwise, the municipality should
appropriate funds for the immediate construction of a new spring box in order that
the waterworks system may be made functional. We further recommended that the
Mayor secure the donation papers for the lot on which the powerhouse was
constructed.

RECOMMENDATION:
We recommended that payment of honoraria in violation of existing rules
and regulations be immediately stopped and that the officials and personnel who
received payment in violation of regulations be required to refund the amounts
collected upon receipt of the Notices of Disallowance. We further recommended
that henceforth, the municipality observe existing rules and regulations on the grant
of honoraria.

RECOMMENDATIONS:
We recommended that management issue demand letters to incumbent and
former officials and employees with unliquidated cash advances otherwise withhold
the amounts from the salaries of incumbent personnel. In addition, require the
Municipal Accountant and other concerned officials/employees to strictly observe
the provisions of COA Circular No. 97-002 in the grant, utilization and liquidation
of cash advances.

RECOMMENDATION:
We recommended that the municipality consider taking on the cost of the
supposed counterpart of the residents by appropriating funds to complete the
project in order that its objectives of providing sanitary toilets and improving the
health and sanitation of their constituents will be achieved.

RECOMMENDATION:
We recommended that the Municipal Engineer carefully review the Program
of Work prepared for each project to ensure reasonable accuracy of not more than
plus or minus ten percent (10%) as stipulated under the Revised IRR of R.A. 9184
in order to avoid excess materials and waste of government funds. We further
recommended that he closely monitor and supervise the implementation of projects
to ensure that these are implemented in accordance with plans and programs of
work.

RECOMMENDATION:
We recommended that in the preparation of the Report of Checks Issued, the
Municipal Treasurer include only those checks which have already been released to
claimants in order to accurately present the amount of disbursements. In addition,
we recommended that the Municipal Accountant take up an adjusting entry for the
unreleased checks which were taken up in the books. Finally, we recommended that
the Municipal Treasurer invariably indicate in the Check Register and on Box D of
the Disbursement voucher the date when the checks were actually released to
claimants for purposes of determination of the actual disbursements as of a given
date.

RECOMMENDATION:
We recommended that the OIC-Municipal Treasurer monitor the
municipality’s depository accounts to ensure that the required minimum balance is
maintained in order to avoid any service charges. We further recommended that
the Municipal Treasurer refund to the municipality the bank service charges of
₱3,630.00.

RECOMMENDATIONS:
We recommended that the municipality immediately avail of the sanitary
landfill facility of LGU-San Carlos City pursuant to the MOA while at the same
time adopt measures to mitigate the effects of its current open dumpsite by fencing
the area and providing protective gears to the laborers assigned to the site. We
further recommended that the municipality make provisions for the eventual phase-
out of the open dumpsite as required by law.

RECOMMENDATION:
We recommended that the Municipal Engineer henceforth ensure that
signboards are installed at the sites of all projects being prosecuted by the
municipality in order that the public is informed of and may keep watch on the
utilization of government resources to help safeguard against loss or wastage.

RECOMMENDATIONS:
We recommended that the Local Finance Committee study and explore
possibilities of increasing local revenues in order to reduce the municipality’s
dependence on the internal revenue allotment to finance its operations. We further
recommended that the Municipal Treasurer impose the taxes on sugar plantations
located in the LGU as provided by law.

We recommended that the: a) Inventory Committee give priority to the physical count guided by the GAAM, Volume I, reconcile the results thereof with the accounting records, prepare the report for submission to our office and pinpoint responsibility for missing properties; b) General Services Officer secure all copies of the ARE and update the filing thereof, ensuring that these are principally considered especially during the separation of accountable officers from the government service; c) Municipal Accountant invariably require that the ARE be fully complied with, in form and quantity, as an indispensable supporting document for purchases of movable PPE; d) Management submit the schedule of PPE additions and disposals from CYs 2014-2016, complete with data on the property tag, ARE, accountable officer, office/location of the equipment, and the actual DVs and ARE; otherwise, those that cannot be accounted for shall be accordingly disallowed; and e) Municipal Accountant attach the formatted and detailed schedule of PPE additions and disposals to the yearly financial report of the LGU and regularly update it to facilitate the conduct of the physical inventory and to pinpoint responsibility for government properties.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to identify the PIs recorded in the various registries that would compose the 25% requirement, prepare the necessary Journal Entry Voucher to facilitate recognition thereof in the books and, henceforth, ensure that the required rate of PI recognition is complied with every year-end until all the PIs are recorded back in the books by the end of CY 2019.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management augment manpower in the Engineering Department and exhaust all means to implement without further delay the projects outlined in the AIP, including the backlogs in prior years, in order to minimize idle funds and maximize the usefulness of such projects. Any favorable action given hereto will ultimately speed up the progress and redound to the benefit of the Municipality and its constituents, and to the achievement of the overall objective of R.A. 7160 to make LGUs self-reliant communities.

 account for the CY 2013 DRRMF totalling P 77,836.66 which could not anymore be traced in the CY 2014 books of accounts.

observations.recommendation:
Recommendations:
We recommended that the:
a) Inventory Committee give priority to the conduct of the physical count guided by
the regulations prescribed under Section 490 of the GAAM, Volume I, reconcile the
results thereof with the accounting records, prepare the report for submission to
our office and pinpoint responsibility for missing properties;
b) General Services Officer secure all copies of the ARE and update the filing thereof,
ensuring that these are principally considered especially during the separation of
accountable officers from the government service;
c) Municipal Accountant invariably require that the ARE be fully complied with, in
form and quantity, as an indispensable supporting document for purchases of
movable PPE;
d) Management submit the schedule of PPE additions and disposals from CYs 2014-
2016, complete with data on the property tag, ARE, accountable officer,
office/location of the equipment, and the actual DVs and ARE; otherwise, those
that cannot be accounted for shall be accordingly disallowed; and
e) Municipal Accountant attach the formatted and detailed schedule of PPE additions
and disposals to the yearly financial report of the LGU and regularly update it to
facilitate the conduct of the physical inventory and to pinpoint responsibility for
government properties.

Recommendation:
We recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to identify the PIs recorded in
the various registries that would compose the 25% requirement, prepare the necessary
Journal Entry Voucher to facilitate recognition thereof in the books and, henceforth,
ensure that the required rate of PI recognition is complied with every year-end until all
the PIs are recorded back in the books by the end of CY 2019.

Recommendation:
We recommended that Management augment the manpower in the Engineering
Department and exhaust all means to implement without further delay the projects
outlined in the AIP, including the backlogs in prior years, in order to minimize idle
funds and maximize the usefulness of such projects. Any favorable action given hereto
will ultimately redound to the benefit of the Municipality and its constituents, and to
the achievement of the overall objective of R.A. 7160 to make LGUs self-reliant
communities.

Recommendation:
We recommended that the LPRAT, through the BUB Focal Person, intensify efforts to
fast-track the implementation of the BUB program by assigning technical personnel
who will focus on BUB projects and by closely monitoring progress thereon for the
LGU to achieve its target of reducing poverty and likewise avert any repercussions that
may result from its low implementation rate. We further recommended that the
Municipal Accountant maintain subsidiary ledgers for all trust receipts, complete with
details and reconciled with controlling accounts, in order to facilitate reporting on
actual fund utilization.

Recommendation:
We recommended and Management, spearheaded by the MENRO and MDRRMO
Designated Officers, agreed to fast-track the preparation of the required plans under
the disaster preparedness component to provide effective guidance in the
implementation of programs outlined therein, and fully undertake measures to comply
with the requirements to pass the SGLG assessment in order to gain access to the PCF
and the incentives package which could provide essential funding for the LGU’s
programs in addressing the eventuality of a disaster.

Recommendation:
We recommended and Management agreed to secure from the Sangguniang Bayan an
appropriation in the General Fund Proper to cover the cost of the motorcycles,
including the pick-up and multicab, in order that the LDRRMF will be restored. We
further recommended that the CY 2016 allocation for two units rescue vehicles
amounting to P1.4million be realigned to other eligible charges to the fund and
henceforth, strictly observe the provisions of NDRRMC-DBM-DILG Joint
Memorandum Circular No. 2013-1 and the Rules and Regulations Implementing R.A.
8185 on the proper utilization of the LDRRMF.

Recommendation:
We recommended and Management agreed to identify, inventory and classify its
insurable properties, submit the report to the GSIS not later than October 31 of each
year, appropriate the necessary premiums in its annual budget under the General
Fund or the 5% LDRRMF, and insure the same with the GIF under the GSIS every
year.

Recommendation:
We recommended and Management agreed to desist from charging to government
funds expenditures related to beauty pageants and exercise caution in the allocation
and utilization of government funds for expenditures related to fiestas or religious
activities, reception and entertainment, ensuring that regulations for the prevention of
irregular and unnecessary expenditures are observed. We further recommend the
immediate refund of the payments made for the beauty pageants as these are
disallowed in audit.

Recommendation:
We recommended and Management agreed to reconstitute without further delay the
LCP Council and the IAMTF so that they could prioritize the formulation of the
LDPC, LIPC and the LSCR and ensure that the plans and budget are adequately
funded, utilized and reported on through the Accomplishment Report for the
attainment of the program’s objectives.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant prepare the necessary adjusting entries to recognize the PPE accounts and to review prior years' transactions for similar omissions in order to maintain the reliability of the financial statements.
We further recommended that the General Services Office prepare and issue the Acknowledgment Receipt for Equipment so as to provide adequate accountability for government properties.
observations.managementAction: Made JEV taking up the ML assets in the TF books.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management:
a) Evaluate the necessity of granting additional service payments to personnel, giving consideration to the source of fund, the PS cap, employment status for entitlement and the requirements of DBM Budget Circular (BC) No. 2007-2 in case of special projects;
b) Formulate and adopt internal rules and procedures on the rendition of overtime services of personnel and ensure that the documentary requirements stipulated under Section 5.10 of COA Circular No. 2012-001 are complied with;
c) Refund the payment of extra time services to personnel simultaneously granted with additional services upon determination that the latter is valid, otherwise refund the payment of the latter; and
d) Submit the following documents to enable us to make a decision in audit:
d.1) legal basis for the grant of additional services to personnel
d.2) certification as to the employment status of the personnel granted with payments for additional services
d.3) PS cost of CY 2015 and compliance with the 55% PS cap
d.4) specific details/Program of Work on the project undertaken for which the daily wages were incurred, and the status of work accomplishment thereon
d.5) explanation on the rendition of extra time services for straight periods and the required documents proving the accomplishment of the expressed necessity.

We recommended that Management exhaust all means to recover from the contractor the 10% retention money equivalent to P400,000.00 which was not deducted from the 50% billing, otherwise, the concerned officials shall be held liable therefor until such could be released upon the expiration of the defects liability period in the absence of any claim to the contrary.
observations.managementAction: Recovered from YCT Builders the amount of P400,000 as 50% retention per OR# 9982179 dated June 6, 2016.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant prepare a JEV reversing the entry made to close the SET Receivable and Deferred SET Income accounts by P599,033.77 and to require the Municipal Treasurer to submit the duly certified list showing the name of taxpayers and the amount due and collectible for the year as basis in accruing the SET receivable in the SEF books of accounts.
observations.managementAction: A reversing entry has been made dated December 29, 2015 JEV#300-15-12-007

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management discontinue deducting from the payroll any payment to the aforementioned private financing institutions, except for loans availed by the employees prior to receipt of this Audit Observation Memorandum until such loans shall have been fully paid and, henceforth, desist from entering into similar contracts with financing institutions not otherwise authorized under the GAA.
observations.reason: the 2 private institutions are no longer granting new loans to the employees.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management to submit the official acknowledgment by the Senior Citizens of the amount of P88,320.00 which was released to them as their share of the La Playa gate entrance fees. We further recommended that accountability for collections made at the La Playa should be directly entrusted to personnel under the Municipal Treasurer’s Office, that cash tickets should be used invariably in exchange for the P3.00 fee collected therefrom, and cease from using unofficial accountable forms in acknowledgment of all funds received by the collectors in their official capacity.
observations.managementAction: Acknowledgment from Senior Citizens already furnished to the audit team.
observations.reason: Implement the use of cash tickets at the La Playa operations.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Sangguniang Bayan, upon recommendation of the Municipal Mayor, enact an ordinance institutionalizing the Local Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Office and appropriate adequate funds necessary for its operation, including the staffing requirements in compliance with NDRRMC-DILG-DBM-CSC Joint Memorandum Circular (JMC) No. 2014-1.
observations.managementAction: The Sanggunian Bayan has passed Ordinance No. 2016-10 creating the LDRRMO on December 13, 2016. LDRRM budget is included in the 2016 2017 budget.
observations.reason: The LDRRMO exists in the plantilla but the position is unfilled since the creation of the plantilla; the creation of the position is currently being questioned by the present administration.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management:
a) Direct the DO to comply with the certification requirements of Section 28 of the NGAS, Volume II and the REM;
b) Instruct the DO to strictly observe the 5-day period within which to liquidate her cash advances for salaries, wages and similar payments and return unexpended balances including unclaimed salaries to the collecting officer who will issue the necessary official receipt. observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management:
a) Adopt measures to mitigate the effects of the current open and/or controlled dumpsite; in compliance with
b) Concretize methods and procedures for the phaseout and the eventual closure of existing open and/or controlled dumps within a specific time frame, and for the establishment of a sanitary landfill in accordance with Sections 40 and 41 of R.A. 9003; and
c) Request the members of the Sangguniang Bayan to allocate funds for the Integrated Solid Waste Management Plan once it is adopted, and give priority to the establishment of a sanitary landfill.
observations.managementAction: Open dumpsites were already closed in compliance with DENR inspection. As an immediate action, the Municipality will enter into a cluster agreement with sanitary landfill of San Carlos City to use its facilities thru a lease agreement.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that: a) The Municipal Mayor closely monitor the LGU’s faithful compliance to the required submission of the accounts and reports; b) The Municipal Accountant maintain a logbook or any other documentary record of the status of submission of accounts by the Municipal Treasurer, reduce into writing any follow-ups made with the latter for compliance to the aforementioned rules, and exert more effort to meet the timelines in the preparation and submission of the accounts and reports; and c) The Municipal Treasurer and Disbursing Officer submit without undue delay to the Municipal Accountant the reports of collections, disbursements and checks issued in accordance with the timelines provided above.
observations.managementAction: The municipality is making all the necessary effort to promptly submit reports.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Mayor require the Municipal Accountant to issue demand letters to all incumbent and previous personnel for the liquidation of their outstanding cash advances, citing the sanctions which will be imposed on them for failure to liquidate, such as withholding of salaries and other emoluments due them, the initiation of administrative proceedings and the filing of malversation charges under Articles 217 and 218 of the Revised Penal Code.
